mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates increasing economic output and capacity utilization, which boosts corporate profits and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors price in stronger earnings.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC of INDPRO's 12-month change versus 20-day forward returns is below 0.03, or the correlation is not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above a threshold of 4.5%, it signals labor market weakness that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10), or the hit rate of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in housing starts and retail sales indicate a rapid contraction in consumer demand that typically precedes recessions, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as growth expectations collapse.
failure_condition: In periods when both HOUST and RSAFS decline MoM, the subsequent 60-day S&P 500 return is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) or the average return is > -0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: During a disinflationary regime in which the year-over-year change in CPI is declining, the Federal Reserve is expected to ease monetary policy, reducing discount rates and boosting equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where CPIAUCSL YoY inflation declines, the average 60-day S&P 500 return is not significantly positive (p > 0.05), or the signal fails to follow the dominant trend at least 60% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment increases and the unemployment rate simultaneously declines, it signals robust labor market momentum that boosts consumer confidence and near-term earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In weeks where PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls MoM, the average 5-day S&P 500 return is not significantly positive (p > 0.10), or the win rate is below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in real retail sales (RSAFS) below 480,000 signals weakening consumer spending that erodes corporate revenues and earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where RSAFS falls below 480,000, the average 20-day S&P 500 return is not negative (p > 0.10), or the negative return hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation regime defined by CPIAUCSL year-over-year above 5%, rising industrial production signals strong demand that allows firms to pass through higher costs, supporting profit margins and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In high inflation months (CPIAUCSL YoY >5%), the Rank IC of INDPRO growth on 20-day forward returns is below 0.03, or the average return during such periods is not positive (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A rising real GDP (quarterly) reflects expanding economic activity and aggregate demand, which supports corporate earnings growth and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days as investors anticipate higher future cash flows.
failure_condition: The correlation between quarterly GDP growth and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05), or the average return following positive GDP prints is less than 1%.
